title: Sarah Paine - Why Russia and China can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing global powers into continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The framework explains how industrial revolution dynamics and geography shape modern motivations like Putin's foreign policy.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a geopolitical framework spanning two thousand years that sorts global powers into two categories: continental empires that seek security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers that seek prosperity through trade (Britain, the United States). The framework ties these motivations to geography and to the dynamics of the industrial revolution. Paine applies it to read modern behavior, including Putin's foreign policy, as a function of the security-through-territory logic rather than a break from historical patterns.
